text:
REFINED BRIEFING (events grouped by probative direction).
Retrieval hints: actors=['Donald Trump', 'White House Press Office'], topics=['press conference', 'White House', 'media relations', 'presidency'].

## BACKGROUND (5)
- [2025-01-10] President-elect Donald Trump received an unconditional discharge for his criminal conviction in New York on Friday, January 10, 2025. The sentence means he will not face fines, prison time, or any other penalties for the 34 felony counts of falsifying business records. Trump appeared virtually in a Manhattan courtroom for the sentencing. New York state Judge Juan Merchan issued the discharge, stating it was the only lawful sentence that would not interfere with the office of the president. Prosecutors from Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg's office had recommended the minimal sentence to ensure finality and respect the jury's verdict. The conviction will remain on Trump's record, and he plans to appeal the decision. The event marked the first time a former, future, or sitting U.S. president has been tried on criminal charges.
